tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome, diabetic nephropathy) or acute illness. Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes less likely. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
